steps.title: Match the graph that is actually shown
steps.content: The graph is not $y=f(x)$; it is $y=f(x)+8$. For a fast SAT check, add $8$ to each answer choice and compare the resulting line to the graph.

steps.title: Read the graph
steps.content: The displayed line clearly passes through $(0,-1)$ and $(1,-6)$. Put those two points into a Desmos table and run $y_1\sim m x_1+b$. The fit gives $m=-5$ and $b=-1$, so the displayed graph is $$y=-5x-1.$$

steps.title: Use the choices
steps.content:
Because $y=f(x)+8$, choice B gives
$$y=(-5x-9)+8=-5x-1$$
which matches the graph. The other choices become lines with either slope $-14$ or y-intercept $16$, so they do not match. Therefore choice B, $f(x)=-5x-9$, is correct.
